last_item: store/instance SaveInstance created_at preservation (internal/store/instance.go:36-56) — the ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET deliberately OMITS created_at so the panel's recorded birth time survives every re-save (panel_version bump on upgrade, label edit, pg_system_id capture), but TestInstanceRoundTrip only ever saved once, so the preserve-on-re-save contract (and that the rest of the row still updates) was unproven; a one-line regression (created_at = excluded.created_at) would silently reset an install's birth time (band-1 correctness)
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): added TestSaveInstancePreservesCreatedAtOnResave + TestSaveInstanceStampsCreatedAtWhenZero to internal/store/store_test.go. Re-save with EVERY field changed AND a different CreatedAt -> assert created_at unchanged (and did NOT adopt the re-save value), every other column updated, COUNT(*)=1. Birth time is supplied in a NON-UTC zone (+05:30) and the test asserts the RAW stored TEXT is canonical UTC (...Z), not the parsed instant (Time.Equal is blind to zone). The zero-value test asserts a zero CreatedAt is stamped now, never 0001-01-01. No bug: the code is correct; the tests lock the contract. Mutation-proven (perl apply + git checkout revert, instance.go restored clean each time): 4 mutations each red the suite — created_at=excluded.created_at in the UPDATE set, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, drop created.UTC() on the write path, drop the if !created.IsZero() guard. Review: code-reviewer clean. test-skeptic found ONE escaping mutation on the first draft (dropping created.UTC() slipped past a UTC-only birth + instant-comparing Time.Equal — a real canonical-UTC-storage regression) and flagged the untested zero-fallback; addressed BOTH before commit (raw-string UTC assertion + zero-value test) and re-verified caught. Gates green (gofmt clean, go vet exit 0, go test ./... exit 0, CGO_ENABLED=0 static build); web untouched; e2e not needed (pure unit).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
